perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, is, and any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of us pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, will be filed promptly. Any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned will be filed only if deemed necessary by a majority vote of the undersigned, notwithstanding Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the requirement for filing amendments. The original text states amendments 'shall be filed', aligning with Rule 13d-1(k). The modified text introduces a condition that amendments are only filed if a majority of the undersigned agree, potentially overriding the mandatory filing requirement of the Securities Exchange Act, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict with the original agreement to comply with Rule 13d-1(k).
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the Schedule 13G/A with respect to the shares of Class A Common Stock, $0.001 par value per share, of The RMR Group Inc., dated as of December 31, 2019, will be filed promptly. Any amendments thereto (including amendments on Schedule 13D) signed by each of the undersigned will be filed only if deemed necessary by a majority vote of the undersigned, notwithstanding Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition that amendments are only filed if a majority of the undersigned agree, potentially overriding the mandatory filing requirement of the Securities Exchange Act, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict with the original agreement to comply with Rule 13d-1(k). This introduces a clear conflict between the obligation to file amendments under the Securities Exchange Act and the discretionary filing based on a majority vote, creating an inconsistency regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
